accordioncompactemits.emits: Emits for the accordion compact component.

members.clearable: Whether selected item can be cleared when `multiple` is false.
members.collapsible:
When type is "single", allows closing content when clicking trigger for an open item.

When type is "multiple", this prop has no effect.
members.contentprops: Props forwarded to the content element.
members.defaultvalue:
The default value of the selected item(s).

Use this when you need to set the initial state of the items.
members.descriptionprops: Props forwarded to the description element.
members.dir: The reading direction of the accordion when applicable. If omitted, assumes LTR (left-to-right) reading mode.
members.disabled: When `true`, prevents the user from interacting with the accordion and all its items
members.headerprops: Props forwarded to the header element.
members.itemprops: Props forwarded to the item element.
members.modelvalue:
The controlled value of the selected item(s).

Use this when you need to control the state of the items. Can be bound with `v-model`
members.multiple:
Determines whether a "single" or "multiple" items can be selected at a time.

This prop will overwrite the inferred type from `modelValue` and `defaultValue`.
members.selectionbehavior: How multiple selection should behave in the collection.
members.triggerprops: Props forwarded to the trigger element.
members.unmountonhide: When `true`, the element will be unmounted on closed state.

callables.cancel: Event handler called when the dialog is requested to be canceled.
callables.closeautofocus: Event handler called when auto-focusing on close. Can be prevented.
callables.confirm: Event handler called when the dialog is requested to be closed by confirming.
callables.escapekeydown: Event handler called when the escape key is down. Can be prevented.
callables.focusoutside: Event handler called when the focus moves outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.
callables.interactoutside:
Event handler called when an interaction happens outside the `DismissableLayer`. Specifically, when a `pointerdown`
event happens outside or focus moves outside of it. Can be prevented.
callables.openautofocus: Event handler called when auto-focusing on open. Can be prevented.
callables.pointerdownoutside: Event handler called when a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.
callables.update_activesnappoint: Emitted when the active snap point value changes.
callables.update_open: Event handler called when the open state of the dialog changes.

members.alerttype: The alert type of the dialog, which determines the default icon and styles when the dialog is an alert dialog.
members.cancelprops: Props forwarded to the cancel element.
members.canceltext: The text of the cancel button. This is used for accessibility purposes and will be rendered in the footer of the dialog if the `cancel` slot is not provided.
members.closeprops: Props forwarded to the close element.
members.confirmprops: Props forwarded to the confirm element.
members.confirmtext: The text of the confirm button. This is used for accessibility purposes and will be rendered in the footer of the dialog if the `confirm` slot is not provided.
members.contentprops: Props forwarded to the content element.
members.defaultopen: The open state of the dialog when it is initially rendered. Use when you do not need to control its open state.
members.description: The description of the dialog. This is used for accessibility purposes and will be rendered in the content of the dialog if the `description` slot is not provided.
members.descriptionprops: Props forwarded to the description element.
members.footerprops: Props forwarded to the footer element.
members.handleprops: Props forwarded to the handle element.
members.headerprops: Props forwarded to the header element.
members.icon: The icon of the dialog. This is used for accessibility purposes and will be rendered in the header of the dialog if the `icon` slot is not provided.
members.isalert:
Whether the dialog is an alert dialog. An alert dialog is a dialog that interrupts the user's workflow to communicate an important message and requires a response.

When set to `true`, the dialog will have `role="alertdialog"` and will require a `DialogTitle` to be provided. This is used for accessibility purposes.
members.modal:
The modality of the dialog. When set to `true`, interaction with outside elements will be disabled and only dialog
content will be visible to screen readers.
members.nobodystyles: When `true` the `body` doesn't get any styles assigned from Vaul
members.open: The controlled open state of the dialog. Can be bound with `v-model:open`.
members.overlayprops: Props forwarded to the overlay element.
members.popupprops: Props forwarded to the popup element.
members.portalprops: Props forwarded to the portal element.
members.pure:
Whether to use the pure version of the dialog, which does not include the header and footer.
This is useful when you want to fully control the content of the dialog and do not need the built-in header and footer.
members.setbackgroundcoloronscale: When `false` we don't change body's background color when the drawer is open.
members.shouldscalebackground: Whether the component should scale background.
members.showcancel:
Whether to show the cancel button.

When set to `onlyWarning`, the cancel button will only be shown when the dialog is an alert dialog with `alertType="warning"`.
When set to `true`, the cancel button will always be shown.
members.showclose: Whether show the close button in the header of the dialog.
members.showconfirm:
Whether to show the confirm button when the dialog is an alert dialog.

The default value is `true` when the dialog is an alert dialog.
members.title: The title of the dialog. This is used for accessibility purposes and will be rendered in the header of the dialog if the `title` slot is not provided.
members.titleprops: Props forwarded to the title element.
members.triggerprops: Props forwarded to the trigger element.

members.to: Route Location the link should navigate to when clicked on.
members.trailingslash:
An option to either add or remove trailing slashes in the `href` for this specific link.

members.viewtransition: Pass the returned promise of `router.push()` to `document.startViewTransition()` if supported.

members.as: The element or component this component should render as. Can be overwrite by `asChild`
members.aschild: Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.
members.clearable: Whether selected item can be cleared when `multiple` is false.
members.defaultvalue:
The default value of the selected item(s).

Use this when you need to set the initial state of the items.
members.dir:
The reading direction of the listbox when applicable.
If omitted, inherits globally from `ConfigProvider` or assumes LTR (left-to-right) reading mode.
members.disabled: When `true`, prevents the user from interacting with listbox
members.highlightonhover: When `true`, hover over item will trigger highlight
members.modelvalue:
The controlled value of the selected item(s).

Use this when you need to control the state of the items. Can be bound with `v-model`
members.multiple:
Determines whether a "single" or "multiple" items can be selected at a time.

This prop will overwrite the inferred type from `modelValue` and `defaultValue`.
members.name: The name of the field. Submitted with its owning form as part of a name/value pair.
members.orientation:
The orientation of the listbox.
Mainly so arrow navigation is done accordingly (left & right vs. up & down)
members.required: When `true`, indicates that the user must set the value before the owning form can be submitted.
members.selectionbehavior: How multiple selection should behave in the collection.

Event handler called when an interaction happens outside the `DismissableLayer`. Specifically, when a `pointerdown`
event happens outside or focus moves outside of it. Can be prevented.
callables.openautofocus: Event handler called when auto-focusing on open. Can be prevented.
callables.pointerdownoutside: Event handler called when a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.

members.placed: Event handler called when the positioner is placed

members.align: The preferred alignment against the trigger. May change when collisions occur.
members.alignflip:
Flip alignment when colliding with boundary.
May only occur when `prioritizePosition` is true.
members.alignoffset: An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options.
members.arrowpadding:
The padding between the arrow and the edges of the content. If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it
from overflowing the corners.
members.avoidcollisions: When `true`, overrides the side and align preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.
members.collisionboundary:
The element used as the collision boundary. By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional
element(s) to be included in this check.
members.collisionpadding:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur. Accepts a number (same for
all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: { top: 20, left: 20 }.
members.disableoutsidepointerevents:
When `true`, hover/focus/click interactions will be disabled on elements outside the `DismissableLayer`. Users will
need to click twice on outside elements to interact with them: once to close the `DismissableLayer`, and again to
trigger the element.
members.disableupdateonlayoutshift: Whether to disable the update position for the content when the layout shifted.
members.forcemount:
Used to force mounting when more control is needed.

Useful when controlling animation with Vue animation libraries.
members.hideshiftedarrow:
When `true`, hides the arrow when it cannot be centered
to the reference element.
members.hidewhendetached: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.
members.placement:
The placement of the floating element.

If used, it will override the `side` and `align` props.
members.popupprops: Props forwarded to the popup element.
members.positionstrategy: The type of CSS position property to use.
members.prioritizeposition: Force content to be position within the viewport. Might overlap the reference element, which may not be desired.
members.reference:
The custom element or virtual element that will be set as the reference to position the floating element. If
provided, it will replace the default anchor element.
members.side:
The preferred side of the trigger to render against when open. Will be reversed when collisions occur and
avoidCollisions is enabled.
members.sideflip: Flip to the opposite side when colliding with boundary.
members.sideoffset: The distance in pixels from the trigger.
members.sticky:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at
least partially in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary regardless.
members.trapfocus: When `true`, focus cannot escape the content via keyboard, pointer, or a programmatic focus.
members.updatepositionstrategy: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

members.modelvalue:

members.resetsearchtermonselect: Whether to reset the searchTerm when the Combobox value is selected

from overflowing the corners.
members.avoidcollisions: When `true`, overrides the side and align preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.
members.collisionboundary:
The element used as the collision boundary. By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional
element(s) to be included in this check.
members.collisionpadding:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur. Accepts a number (same for
all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: { top: 20, left: 20 }.
members.disableupdateonlayoutshift: Whether to disable the update position for the content when the layout shifted.
members.forcemount:
Used to force mounting when more control is needed.

Useful when controlling animation with Vue animation libraries.
members.hideshiftedarrow:
When `true`, hides the arrow when it cannot be centered
to the reference element.
members.hidewhendetached: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.
members.placement:
The placement of the floating element.

If used, it will override the `side` and `align` props.
members.popupprops: Props forwarded to the popup element.
members.portalprops: Props forwarded to the portal element.
members.positionstrategy: The type of CSS position property to use.
members.prioritizeposition: Force content to be position within the viewport. Might overlap the reference element, which may not be desired.
members.reference:
The custom element or virtual element that will be set as the reference to position the floating element. If
provided, it will replace the default anchor element.
members.side:
The preferred side of the trigger to render against when open. Will be reversed when collisions occur and
avoidCollisions is enabled.
members.sideflip: Flip to the opposite side when colliding with boundary.
members.sideoffset: The distance in pixels from the trigger.
members.sticky:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at
least partially in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary regardless.
members.updatepositionstrategy: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

members.alerttype: The alert type of the dialog, which determines the default icon and styles when the dialog is an alert dialog.
members.cancelprops: Props forwarded to the cancel element.
members.canceltext: The text of the cancel button. This is used for accessibility purposes and will be rendered in the footer of the dialog if the `cancel` slot is not provided.
members.closeprops: Props forwarded to the close element.
members.confirmprops: Props forwarded to the confirm element.
members.confirmtext: The text of the confirm button. This is used for accessibility purposes and will be rendered in the footer of the dialog if the `confirm` slot is not provided.
members.contentprops: Props forwarded to the content element.
members.defaultopen: The open state of the dialog when it is initially rendered. Use when you do not need to control its open state.
members.description: The description of the dialog. This is used for accessibility purposes and will be rendered in the content of the dialog if the `description` slot is not provided.
members.descriptionprops: Props forwarded to the description element.
members.footerprops: Props forwarded to the footer element.
members.headerprops: Props forwarded to the header element.
members.icon: The icon of the dialog. This is used for accessibility purposes and will be rendered in the header of the dialog if the `icon` slot is not provided.
members.isalert:
Whether the dialog is an alert dialog. An alert dialog is a dialog that interrupts the user's workflow to communicate an important message and requires a response.

When set to `true`, the dialog will have `role="alertdialog"` and will require a `DialogTitle` to be provided. This is used for accessibility purposes.
members.modal:
The modality of the dialog. When set to `true`, interaction with outside elements will be disabled and only dialog
content will be visible to screen readers.
members.open: The controlled open state of the dialog. Can be bound with `v-model:open`.
members.overlayprops: Props forwarded to the overlay element.
members.popupprops: Props forwarded to the popup element.
members.portalprops: Props forwarded to the portal element.
members.pure:
Whether to use the pure version of the dialog, which does not include the header and footer.
This is useful when you want to fully control the content of the dialog and do not need the built-in header and footer.
members.showcancel:
Whether to show the cancel button.

When set to `onlyWarning`, the cancel button will only be shown when the dialog is an alert dialog with `alertType="warning"`.
When set to `true`, the cancel button will always be shown.
members.showclose: Whether show the close button in the header of the dialog.
members.showconfirm:
Whether to show the confirm button when the dialog is an alert dialog.

The default value is `true` when the dialog is an alert dialog.
members.title: The title of the dialog. This is used for accessibility purposes and will be rendered in the header of the dialog if the `title` slot is not provided.
members.titleprops: Props forwarded to the title element.
members.triggerprops: Props forwarded to the trigger element.

from overflowing the corners.

members.collisionboundary:
The element used as the collision boundary. By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional
element(s) to be included in this check.
members.collisionpadding:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur. Accepts a number (same for
all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: { top: 20, left: 20 }.
members.disableupdateonlayoutshift: Whether to disable the update position for the content when the layout shifted.

Used to force mounting when more control is needed.

Useful when controlling animation with Vue animation libraries.
members.hideshiftedarrow:
When `true`, hides the arrow when it cannot be centered

members.placement:
The placement of the floating element.

members.popupprops: Props forwarded to the popup element.
members.positionstrategy: The type of CSS position property to use.
members.prioritizeposition: Force content to be position within the viewport. Might overlap the reference element, which may not be desired.
members.reference:
The custom element or virtual element that will be set as the reference to position the floating element. If

members.align: The preferred alignment against the trigger. May change when collisions occur.
members.alignflip:
Flip alignment when colliding with boundary.
May only occur when `prioritizePosition` is true.
members.alignoffset: An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options.
members.arrowpadding:
The padding between the arrow and the edges of the content. If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it
from overflowing the corners.
members.avoidcollisions: When `true`, overrides the side and align preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.
members.collisionboundary:
The element used as the collision boundary. By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional
element(s) to be included in this check.
members.collisionpadding:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur. Accepts a number (same for
all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: { top: 20, left: 20 }.
members.disableupdateonlayoutshift: Whether to disable the update position for the content when the layout shifted.
members.forcemount:
Used to force mounting when more control is needed.

Useful when controlling animation with Vue animation libraries.
members.hideshiftedarrow:
When `true`, hides the arrow when it cannot be centered
to the reference element.
members.hidewhendetached: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.
members.placement:
The placement of the floating element.

If used, it will override the `side` and `align` props.
members.positionstrategy: The type of CSS position property to use.
members.prioritizeposition: Force content to be position within the viewport. Might overlap the reference element, which may not be desired.
members.reference:
The custom element or virtual element that will be set as the reference to position the floating element. If
provided, it will replace the default anchor element.
members.side:
The preferred side of the trigger to render against when open. Will be reversed when collisions occur and
avoidCollisions is enabled.
members.sideflip: Flip to the opposite side when colliding with boundary.
members.sideoffset: The distance in pixels from the trigger.
members.sticky:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at
least partially in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary regardless.
members.updatepositionstrategy: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

The default value of the selected item(s).

Use this when you need to set the initial state of the items.

The reading direction of the listbox when applicable.
If omitted, inherits globally from `ConfigProvider` or assumes LTR (left-to-right) reading mode.
members.disabled: When `true`, prevents the user from interacting with listbox
members.highlightonhover: When `true`, hover over item will trigger highlight
members.modelvalue:
The controlled value of the selected item(s).

Use this when you need to control the state of the items. Can be bound with `v-model`
members.multiple:
Determines whether a "single" or "multiple" items can be selected at a time.

This prop will overwrite the inferred type from `modelValue` and `defaultValue`.
members.name: The name of the field. Submitted with its owning form as part of a name/value pair.
members.orientation:
The orientation of the listbox.
Mainly so arrow navigation is done accordingly (left & right vs. up & down)
members.required: When `true`, indicates that the user must set the value before the owning form can be submitted.
members.selectionbehavior: How multiple selection should behave in the collection.

members.align: The preferred alignment against the trigger. May change when collisions occur.
members.alignflip:
Flip alignment when colliding with boundary.
May only occur when `prioritizePosition` is true.
members.alignoffset: An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options.
members.arrowpadding:
The padding between the arrow and the edges of the content. If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it
from overflowing the corners.
members.avoidcollisions: When `true`, overrides the side and align preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.
members.collisionboundary:
The element used as the collision boundary. By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional
element(s) to be included in this check.
members.collisionpadding:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur. Accepts a number (same for
all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: { top: 20, left: 20 }.
members.disableupdateonlayoutshift: Whether to disable the update position for the content when the layout shifted.
members.forcemount:
Used to force mounting when more control is needed.

Useful when controlling animation with Vue animation libraries.
members.hideshiftedarrow:
When `true`, hides the arrow when it cannot be centered
to the reference element.
members.hidewhendetached: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.
members.loop: Whether keyboard navigation should loop around
members.placement:
The placement of the floating element.

If used, it will override the `side` and `align` props.
members.popupprops: Props forwarded to the popup element.
members.positionstrategy: The type of CSS position property to use.
members.prioritizeposition: Force content to be position within the viewport. Might overlap the reference element, which may not be desired.
members.reference:
The custom element or virtual element that will be set as the reference to position the floating element. If
provided, it will replace the default anchor element.
members.side:
The preferred side of the trigger to render against when open. Will be reversed when collisions occur and
avoidCollisions is enabled.
members.sideflip: Flip to the opposite side when colliding with boundary.
members.sideoffset: The distance in pixels from the trigger.
members.sticky:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at
least partially in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary regardless.
members.updatepositionstrategy: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

members.align: The preferred alignment against the trigger. May change when collisions occur.
members.alignflip:
Flip alignment when colliding with boundary.
May only occur when `prioritizePosition` is true.
members.alignoffset: An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options.
members.arrowpadding:
The padding between the arrow and the edges of the content. If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it
from overflowing the corners.
members.avoidcollisions: When `true`, overrides the side and align preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.
members.collisionboundary:
The element used as the collision boundary. By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional
element(s) to be included in this check.
members.collisionpadding:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur. Accepts a number (same for
all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: { top: 20, left: 20 }.
members.disableupdateonlayoutshift: Whether to disable the update position for the content when the layout shifted.
members.forcemount:
Used to force mounting when more control is needed.

Useful when controlling animation with Vue animation libraries.
members.hideshiftedarrow:
When `true`, hides the arrow when it cannot be centered
to the reference element.
members.hidewhendetached: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.
members.loop: Whether keyboard navigation should loop around
members.placement:
The placement of the floating element.

If used, it will override the `side` and `align` props.
members.popupprops: Props forwarded to the popup element.
members.positionstrategy: The type of CSS position property to use.
members.prioritizeposition: Force content to be position within the viewport. Might overlap the reference element, which may not be desired.
members.reference:
The custom element or virtual element that will be set as the reference to position the floating element. If
provided, it will replace the default anchor element.
members.side:
The preferred side of the trigger to render against when open. Will be reversed when collisions occur and
avoidCollisions is enabled.
members.sideflip: Flip to the opposite side when colliding with boundary.
members.sideoffset: The distance in pixels from the trigger.
members.sticky:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at
least partially in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary regardless.
members.updatepositionstrategy: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

members.align: The preferred alignment against the trigger. May change when collisions occur.
members.alignflip:
Flip alignment when colliding with boundary.
May only occur when `prioritizePosition` is true.
members.alignoffset: An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options.
members.arrowpadding:
The padding between the arrow and the edges of the content. If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it
from overflowing the corners.
members.avoidcollisions: When `true`, overrides the side and align preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.
members.collisionboundary:
The element used as the collision boundary. By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional
element(s) to be included in this check.
members.collisionpadding:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur. Accepts a number (same for
all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: { top: 20, left: 20 }.
members.disableupdateonlayoutshift: Whether to disable the update position for the content when the layout shifted.
members.forcemount:
Used to force mounting when more control is needed.

Useful when controlling animation with Vue animation libraries.
members.hideshiftedarrow:
When `true`, hides the arrow when it cannot be centered
to the reference element.
members.hidewhendetached: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.
members.placement:
The placement of the floating element.

If used, it will override the `side` and `align` props.
members.positionstrategy: The type of CSS position property to use.
members.prioritizeposition: Force content to be position within the viewport. Might overlap the reference element, which may not be desired.
members.reference:
The custom element or virtual element that will be set as the reference to position the floating element. If
provided, it will replace the default anchor element.
members.side:
The preferred side of the trigger to render against when open. Will be reversed when collisions occur and
avoidCollisions is enabled.
members.sideflip: Flip to the opposite side when colliding with boundary.
members.sideoffset: The distance in pixels from the trigger.
members.sticky:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at
least partially in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary regardless.
members.updatepositionstrategy: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

members.disableoutsidepointerevents:

members.position:
The positioning mode to use `item-aligned (default)` - behaves similarly to a native MacOS menu by positioning content relative to the active item.

The default value of the selected item(s).

members.dir:
The reading direction of the combobox when applicable.

members.modelvalue:

members.multiple:
Determines whether a "single" or "multiple" items can be selected at a time.

This prop will overwrite the inferred type from `modelValue` and `defaultValue`.

members.norel: If set to true, no rel attribute will be added to the link

members.prefetchon: Allows controlling when to prefetch links. By default, prefetch is triggered only on visibility.
members.rel: A rel attribute value to apply on the link. Defaults to "noopener noreferrer" for external links.
members.target: Where to display the linked URL, as the name for a browsing context.
members.to: Route Location the link should navigate to when clicked on.
members.trailingslash:
An option to either add or remove trailing slashes in the `href` for this specific link.
Overrides the global `trailingSlash` option if provided.

tooltippositioneremits.emits: Emits for the tooltip positioner component.

callables.escapekeydown: Event handler called when the escape key is down. Can be prevented.
callables.pointerdownoutside: Event handler called when a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.

members.align: The preferred alignment against the trigger. May change when collisions occur.
members.alignflip:
Flip alignment when colliding with boundary.
May only occur when `prioritizePosition` is true.
members.alignoffset: An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options.
members.arrowpadding:
The padding between the arrow and the edges of the content. If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it
from overflowing the corners.
members.avoidcollisions: When `true`, overrides the side and align preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.
members.collisionboundary:
The element used as the collision boundary. By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional
element(s) to be included in this check.
members.collisionpadding:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur. Accepts a number (same for
all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: { top: 20, left: 20 }.
members.disableupdateonlayoutshift: Whether to disable the update position for the content when the layout shifted.
members.forcemount:
Used to force mounting when more control is needed.

Useful when controlling animation with Vue animation libraries.
members.hideshiftedarrow:
When `true`, hides the arrow when it cannot be centered
to the reference element.
members.hidewhendetached: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.
members.placement:
The placement of the floating element.

If used, it will override the `side` and `align` props.
members.positionstrategy: The type of CSS position property to use.
members.prioritizeposition: Force content to be position within the viewport. Might overlap the reference element, which may not be desired.
members.reference:
The custom element or virtual element that will be set as the reference to position the floating element. If
provided, it will replace the default anchor element.
members.side:
The preferred side of the trigger to render against when open. Will be reversed when collisions occur and
avoidCollisions is enabled.
members.sideflip: Flip to the opposite side when colliding with boundary.
members.sideoffset: The distance in pixels from the trigger.
members.sticky:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at
least partially in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary regardless.
members.updatepositionstrategy: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

members.delayduration: The duration from when the pointer enters the trigger until the tooltip gets opened.
members.disableclosingtrigger: When `true`, clicking on trigger will not close the content.
members.disabled: When `true`, disable tooltip
members.disablehoverablecontent: When `true`, trying to hover the content will result in the tooltip closing as the pointer leaves the trigger.
members.ignorenonkeyboardfocus:
Prevent the tooltip from opening if the focus did not come from the keyboard by matching against the
`:focus-visible` selector. This is useful if you want to avoid opening it when switching browser tabs or closing a
dialog.
members.positionerprops: Props to be passed down to the positioner. Useful when you need to control the positioner, such as disabling it when the trigger is disabled.
members.skipdelayduration: How much time a user has to enter another trigger without incurring a delay again.

node_modules_pnpm_typescript_6_0_3_node_.clipboardevent:
The **`ClipboardEvent`** interface of the Clipboard API represents events providing information related to modification of the clipboard, that is cut, copy, and paste events.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/ClipboardEvent)
node_modules_pnpm_typescript_6_0_3_node_.customevent:
The **`CustomEvent`** interface can be used to attach custom data to an event generated by an application.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/CustomEvent)
node_modules_pnpm_typescript_6_0_3_node_.element:
**`Element`** is the most general base class from which all element objects (i.e., objects that represent elements) in a Document inherit. It only has methods and properties common to all kinds of elements. More specific classes inherit from Element.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Element)
node_modules_pnpm_typescript_6_0_3_node_.event:
The **`Event`** interface represents an event which takes place on an EventTarget.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Event)
node_modules_pnpm_typescript_6_0_3_node_.focusevent:
The **`FocusEvent`** interface represents focus-related events, including focus, blur, focusin, and focusout.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/FocusEvent)
node_modules_pnpm_typescript_6_0_3_node_.htmlelement:
The **`HTMLElement`** interface represents any HTML element. Some elements directly implement this interface, while others implement it via an interface that inherits it.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/HTMLElement)
node_modules_pnpm_typescript_6_0_3_node_.htmlinputelement:
The **`HTMLInputElement`** interface provides special properties and methods for manipulating the options, layout, and presentation of <input> elements.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/HTMLInputElement)
node_modules_pnpm_typescript_6_0_3_node_.htmltextareaelement:
The **`HTMLTextAreaElement`** interface provides properties and methods for manipulating the layout and presentation of <textarea> elements.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/HTMLTextAreaElement)
node_modules_pnpm_typescript_6_0_3_node_.keyboardevent:
**`KeyboardEvent`** objects describe a user interaction with the keyboard; each event describes a single interaction between the user and a key (or combination of a key with modifier keys) on the keyboard. The event type (keydown, keypress, or keyup) identifies what kind of keyboard activity occurred.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/KeyboardEvent)
node_modules_pnpm_typescript_6_0_3_node_.mouseevent:
The **`MouseEvent`** interface represents events that occur due to the user interacting with a pointing device (such as a mouse). Common events using this interface include click, dblclick, mouseup, mousedown.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/MouseEvent)
node_modules_pnpm_typescript_6_0_3_node_.pointerevent:
The **`PointerEvent`** interface represents the state of a DOM event produced by a pointer such as the geometry of the contact point, the device type that generated the event, the amount of pressure that was applied on the contact surface, etc.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/PointerEvent)

members.confirmprops: Props forwarded to the confirm element.

members.contentprops: Props forwarded to the content element.
members.defaultopen: The open state of the dialog when it is initially rendered. Use when you do not need to control its open state.
members.description: The description of the dialog. This is used for accessibility purposes and will be rendered in the content of the dialog if the `description` slot is not provided.
members.descriptionprops: Props forwarded to the description element.
members.footerprops: Props forwarded to the footer element.
members.handleprops: Props forwarded to the handle element.
members.headerprops: Props forwarded to the header element.
members.icon: The icon of the dialog. This is used for accessibility purposes and will be rendered in the header of the dialog if the `icon` slot is not provided.
members.isalert:
Whether the dialog is an alert dialog. An alert dialog is a dialog that interrupts the user's workflow to communicate an important message and requires a response.

When set to `true`, the dialog will have `role="alertdialog"` and will require a `DialogTitle` to be provided. This is used for accessibility purposes.
members.modal:
The modality of the dialog. When set to `true`, interaction with outside elements will be disabled and only dialog
content will be visible to screen readers.
members.nobodystyles: When `true` the `body` doesn't get any styles assigned from Vaul
members.open: The controlled open state of the dialog. Can be bound with `v-model:open`.
members.overlayprops: Props forwarded to the overlay element.
members.popupprops: Props forwarded to the popup element.
members.portalprops: Props forwarded to the portal element.
members.pure:
Whether to use the pure version of the dialog, which does not include the header and footer.
This is useful when you want to fully control the content of the dialog and do not need the built-in header and footer.
members.setbackgroundcoloronscale: When `false` we don't change body's background color when the drawer is open.
members.shouldscalebackground: Whether the component should scale background.
members.showcancel:
Whether to show the cancel button.

When set to `onlyWarning`, the cancel button will only be shown when the dialog is an alert dialog with `alertType="warning"`.
When set to `true`, the cancel button will always be shown.
members.showclose: Whether show the close button in the header of the dialog.
members.showconfirm:
Whether to show the confirm button when the dialog is an alert dialog.

The default value is `true` when the dialog is an alert dialog.
members.title: The title of the dialog. This is used for accessibility purposes and will be rendered in the header of the dialog if the `title` slot is not provided.
members.titleprops: Props forwarded to the title element.
members.triggerprops: Props forwarded to the trigger element.
members.ui: Per-slot class overrides for the component.

callables.cancel: Event handler called when the dialog is requested to be canceled.
callables.close: Event handler called when the dialog is requested to be closed.
callables.closeautofocus: Event handler called when auto-focusing on close. Can be prevented.
callables.confirm: Event handler called when the dialog is requested to be closed by confirming.
callables.escapekeydown: Event handler called when the escape key is down. Can be prevented.
callables.focusoutside: Event handler called when the focus moves outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.
callables.interactoutside:
Event handler called when an interaction happens outside the `DismissableLayer`. Specifically, when a `pointerdown`
event happens outside or focus moves outside of it. Can be prevented.
callables.openautofocus: Event handler called when auto-focusing on open. Can be prevented.
callables.pointerdownoutside: Event handler called when a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.
callables.update_open: Event handler called when the open state of the dialog changes.

members.alerttype: The alert type of the dialog, which determines the default icon and styles when the dialog is an alert dialog.
members.cancelprops: Props forwarded to the cancel element.
members.canceltext: The text of the cancel button. This is used for accessibility purposes and will be rendered in the footer of the dialog if the `cancel` slot is not provided.
members.class: the popup class of the dialog
members.closeprops: Props forwarded to the close element.
members.confirmprops: Props forwarded to the confirm element.
members.confirmtext: The text of the confirm button. This is used for accessibility purposes and will be rendered in the footer of the dialog if the `confirm` slot is not provided.
members.contentprops: Props forwarded to the content element.
members.defaultopen: The open state of the dialog when it is initially rendered. Use when you do not need to control its open state.
members.description: The description of the dialog. This is used for accessibility purposes and will be rendered in the content of the dialog if the `description` slot is not provided.
members.descriptionprops: Props forwarded to the description element.
members.footerprops: Props forwarded to the footer element.
members.headerprops: Props forwarded to the header element.
members.icon: The icon of the dialog. This is used for accessibility purposes and will be rendered in the header of the dialog if the `icon` slot is not provided.
members.isalert:
Whether the dialog is an alert dialog. An alert dialog is a dialog that interrupts the user's workflow to communicate an important message and requires a response.

When set to `true`, the dialog will have `role="alertdialog"` and will require a `DialogTitle` to be provided. This is used for accessibility purposes.
members.modal:
The modality of the dialog. When set to `true`, interaction with outside elements will be disabled and only dialog
content will be visible to screen readers.
members.open: The controlled open state of the dialog. Can be bound with `v-model:open`.
members.overlayprops: Props forwarded to the overlay element.
members.popupprops: Props forwarded to the popup element.
members.portalprops: Props forwarded to the portal element.
members.pure:
Whether to use the pure version of the dialog, which does not include the header and footer.
This is useful when you want to fully control the content of the dialog and do not need the built-in header and footer.
members.showcancel:
Whether to show the cancel button.

When set to `onlyWarning`, the cancel button will only be shown when the dialog is an alert dialog with `alertType="warning"`.
When set to `true`, the cancel button will always be shown.
members.showclose: Whether show the close button in the header of the dialog.
members.showconfirm:
Whether to show the confirm button when the dialog is an alert dialog.

The default value is `true` when the dialog is an alert dialog.
members.title: The title of the dialog. This is used for accessibility purposes and will be rendered in the header of the dialog if the `title` slot is not provided.
members.titleprops: Props forwarded to the title element.
members.triggerprops: Props forwarded to the trigger element.
members.ui: Per-slot class overrides for the component.

members.class: class of popup
members.defaultopen: The open state of the tooltip when it is initially rendered. Use when you do not need to control its open state.
members.delayduration: The duration from when the pointer enters the trigger until the tooltip gets opened.
members.disableclosingtrigger: When `true`, clicking on trigger will not close the content.
members.disabled: When `true`, disable tooltip
members.disablehoverablecontent: When `true`, trying to hover the content will result in the tooltip closing as the pointer leaves the trigger.
members.ignorenonkeyboardfocus:
Prevent the tooltip from opening if the focus did not come from the keyboard by matching against the
`:focus-visible` selector. This is useful if you want to avoid opening it when switching browser tabs or closing a
dialog.
members.open: The controlled open state of the tooltip.
members.popupprops: Props forwarded to the popup element.
members.portalprops: Props forwarded to the portal element.
members.positionerprops: Props to be passed down to the positioner. Useful when you need to control the positioner, such as disabling it when the trigger is disabled.
members.skipdelayduration: How much time a user has to enter another trigger without incurring a delay again.
members.triggerprops: Props forwarded to the trigger element.
members.ui: Per-slot class overrides for the component.
